document.id: Upper Mattaponi Tribal Code of Law
document.heading: Upper Mattaponi Tribal Code of Law

container.heading: The Land Office Establishment and Land Acquisition Act of 2025

section.heading: Short Title
section.text: This enactment shall be known as, "The Land Office Establishment and Land Acquisition Act of 2025" ("Act").

section.heading: Purpose
section.text: The purpose of this Act is to establish a Land Office to reacquire and restore lands to the Tribe, and to address other land-related matters for the Tribe as authorized by the Chief.

para.text: The Tribe has current and future needs for real property for such purposes as housing, community development, government facilities and infrastructure, protection of historic and cultural resources, economic development, and restoration of the Tribe's territory; and,

The federal Thomasina E. Jordan Indian Tribes of Virginia Federal Recognition Act of 2017, specifically recognizes the right of the Tribe to acquire land, and to place such land into trust, within five listed counties within the State of Virginia; and,

The Council hereby finds and declares that it is in the best interest of the Tribe to establish a Land Office to acquire additional lands to expand the Tribe's territory for the best interests of the Tribe and its citizens.
There is hereby established a Department of Land, which shall be known as the Land Office ("Land Office"). The Land Office shall be located with the Executive branch of government.

The Director of the Land Office shall have sufficient experience in land and real estate matters to properly evaluate the Tribe's land acquisition efforts and to provide professional advice on land acquisition, land use, and related matters.

The Land Office shall develop, plan, and coordinate activities associated with the Tribe's existing lands and the acquisition of additional lands by the Tribe. The Land Office is hereby authorized to purchase available lands or interests in land, subject to the appropriation of funds in an approved Annual Budget or budget modification for such purposes.
The Land Office shall develop a short-term plan and a long-term plan for the use of existing lands and the acquisition of additional lands by the Tribe.

The Land Office shall take all steps necessary to participate in, to the fullest extent possible, and to take advantage of the benefits of, the federal land buyback program, and in any other federal or private programs or services to assist with the acquisition of lands by the Tribe.
The Council hereby authorizes the establishment of the Land Office, subject to an appropriation of funds in an Annual Budget or Budget Modification for such purpose.

container.heading: Emergency Assistance Fund Authorization Act of 2024

section.heading: Short Title
section.text: This enactment shall be known as the "EMERGENCY ASSISTANCE FUND AUTHORIZATION ACT OF 2024" ("Act").

section.heading: Purpose
section.text: The purpose of this Act is to provide emergency funds to Citizens of the Tribe upon request for family, medical, and other personal needs; however, no written standards exist for determining reasons why, and in what amount, emergency funds should be issued.

section.heading: Findings
section.text: The Council hereby finds and declares that:

para.text: On July 22, 2023, the voters of the Tribe approved a new constitution ("New Constitution");
The New Constitution calls for the Council to pass laws and resolutions to establish clear financial practices that the must be followed to provide accountability of the Tribe' funds and assets;
The Bill of Rights in the Constitution requires that the Tribe to provide equal treatment to all Citizens of the Tribe when it administers programs and services.

section.heading: Minimum Criteria
section.text: The Council hereby authorizes and directs the Chief, or his designee, to develop and implement written rules and procedures to administer the Emergency Assistance Fund for distribution of funds to Citizens of the Tribe or on behalf of Citizens of the Tribe, which shall include the following minimum criteria:

para.text: The rules shall establish a fair and equal process for Citizens of the Tribe to apply for and receive appropriated funds from the Emergency Assistance Fund for emergency purposes.

Each distribution from the Emergency Assistance Fund shall be limited up to an amount indicated per person as appropriated in an approved Annual Budget, until the appropriated funds have been expended.

The funds from the Emergency Assistance Fund shall be divided into four equal quarters each fiscal year and shall be issued on a first-come, first-served basis until all of the appropriated funds are expended from the Emergency Assistance Fund for each quarter of the fiscal year; provided, that unused funds may be utilized in the next quarter of the fiscal year.

The Chief or his designee shall be required to maintain a written record of all funds issued from the Emergency Assistance Fund, the age of the Citizen to whom the funds were issued, and the reason offered in support of the request for funds, and to provide a copy of the report to the Council no later than sixty days after the end of each fiscal year.

section.heading: Authorization of Appropriation of Funds
section.text: The Council hereby authorizes an appropriation of funds to be included in the Annual Budget or Budget Modification for expenditure to implement this Act.
